steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: Note $x^2y-8xy=xy(x-8)$. Get the common denominator and combine:$$\frac{(y+12)\cdot xy}{x^2y-8xy}+\frac{y(x-8)}{x^2y-8xy}=\frac{xy^2+12xy+xy-8y}{x^2y-8xy}=\frac{xy^2+13xy-8y}{x^2y-8xy}$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $\dfrac{xy^2+13xy-8y}{x^2y-8xy}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
